description: This figure presents an Ashby-style materials selection map plotting specific hardness (hardness/density, HV·cm³/g) against density (g/cm³) to compare Fe-Mn-Al-Cu low-density steels against existing material classes. The scatter plot displays data clusters for three alloy variants (15Al, 17Al, and 19Al, representing Fe-35Mn-xAl-5Cu compositions) shown as colored point groups in the low-density region (~6.6–7.0 g/cm³), achieving ultra-high specific hardness values exceeding 140 HV·cm³/g. These are contrasted with other low-density steels (orange circles), conventional steels including stainless and high-speed steels (pink squares), pure iron (black star at ~7.8 g/cm³), and medium/high entropy alloys M/HEAs (blue hexagons), which predominantly occupy higher density regions (>7.5 g/cm³) with specific hardness below 120 HV·cm³/g (indicated by a dashed reference line). The 15Al alloy demonstrates the optimal performance, reaching specific hardness of ~140 HV·cm³/g while maintaining fracture toughness of 8.6 MPa·m^1/2. The figure establishes that the developed Fe-Mn-Al-Cu steels significantly outperform existing steels and M/HEAs, breaking through the typical ~120 HV·cm³/g specific hardness limit while maintaining low density.
